title: porophyllum ruderale var. macrocephalum
intro: Porophyllum ruderale var. macrocephalum, also known as Mexican tarragon or Yerba anis, is a plant species belonging to the asteraceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and tall, branching stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and carminative effects, making it a popular remedy for digestive issues and other health problems. The health benefits of Mexican tarragon include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive discomfort, and supporting immune function, among others. Herbal preparations of Porophyllum ruderale var. macrocephalum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal remedies and flavor food and drinks.
